ayahArabic: وَٱلذَّارِيٰتِ ذَرْوًا
ayahEnglish: By the [winds] scattering [dust], dispersing [it]

targetTranslation: a heavy load (object being carried by الحاملات)
ayahArabic: فَٱلْحٰمِلٰتِ وِقْرًا
ayahEnglish: And the [clouds] carrying a load [of water]

ayahArabic: فَٱلْجٰرِيٰتِ يُسْرًا
ayahEnglish: And the ships sailing with ease

targetTranslation: a command/matter/affair (object of فَٱلْمُقَسِّمٰتِ - those who distribute)
ayahArabic: فَٱلْمُقَسِّمٰتِ أَمْرًا
ayahEnglish: And the [angels] apportioning [each] matter,

targetTranslation: you (all) are promised/warned (what is mentioned in this surah)
ayahArabic: إِنَّمَا تُوعَدُونَ لَصَادِقٌ
ayahEnglish: Indeed, what you are promised is true.

targetTranslation: possessor of (describing السَّمَاء, owned by الْحُبُكِ)
ayahArabic: وَٱلسَّمَآءِ ذَاتِ ٱلْحُبُكِ
ayahEnglish: By the heaven containing pathways,

ayahArabic: إِنَّكُمْ لَفِى قَوْلٍ مُّخْتَلِفٍ
ayahEnglish: Indeed, you are in differing speech.

ayahArabic: إِنَّكُمْ لَفِى قَوْلٍ مُّخْتَلِفٍ
ayahEnglish: Indeed, you are in differing speech.

ayahArabic: يُؤْفَكُ عَنْهُ مَنْ أُفِكَ
ayahEnglish: Deluded away from it [i.e., the Qur’ān] is he who is deluded.

ayahArabic: يُؤْفَكُ عَنْهُ مَنْ أُفِكَ
ayahEnglish: Deluded away from it [i.e., the Qur’ān] is he who is deluded.

targetTranslation: he was turned away (subject of يُؤْفَكُ)
ayahArabic: يُؤْفَكُ عَنْهُ مَنْ أُفِكَ
ayahEnglish: Deluded away from it [i.e., the Qur’ān] is he who is deluded.

targetTranslation: he was killed (subject of this statement is ٱلْخَرَّاصُونَ - the liars)
ayahArabic: قُتِلَ ٱلْخَرَّاصُونَ
ayahEnglish: Destroyed are the misinformers

ayahArabic: ٱلَّذِينَ هُمْ فِى غَمْرَةٍ سَاهُونَ
ayahEnglish: Who are within a flood [of confusion] and heedless.

ayahArabic: ٱلَّذِينَ هُمْ فِى غَمْرَةٍ سَاهُونَ
ayahEnglish: Who are within a flood [of confusion] and heedless.

targetTranslation: in a state of being overwhelmed (describes حال of الَّذِينَ)
ayahArabic: ٱلَّذِينَ هُمْ فِى غَمْرَةٍ سَاهُونَ
ayahEnglish: Who are within a flood [of confusion] and heedless.

ayahArabic: ٱلَّذِينَ هُمْ فِى غَمْرَةٍ سَاهُونَ
ayahEnglish: Who are within a flood [of confusion] and heedless.

targetTranslation: they ask (about أَيَّانَ يَوْمُ ٱلدِّينِ - when is the Day of Judgment)
ayahArabic: يَسْـَٔلُونَ أَيَّانَ يَوْمُ ٱلدِّينِ
ayahEnglish: They ask, "When is the Day of Recompense?"

ayahArabic: يَسْـَٔلُونَ أَيَّانَ يَوْمُ ٱلدِّينِ
ayahEnglish: They ask, "When is the Day of Recompense?"

ayahArabic: يَسْـَٔلُونَ أَيَّانَ يَوْمُ ٱلدِّينِ
ayahEnglish: They ask, "When is the Day of Recompense?"

targetTranslation: on the day when (they will be tested)
ayahArabic: يَوْمَ هُمْ عَلَى ٱلنَّارِ يُفْتَنُونَ
ayahEnglish: [It is] the Day they will be tormented over the Fire.

ayahArabic: يَوْمَ هُمْ عَلَى ٱلنَّارِ يُفْتَنُونَ
ayahEnglish: [It is] the Day they will be tormented over the Fire.

targetTranslation: they are being tested (on the Fire)
ayahArabic: يَوْمَ هُمْ عَلَى ٱلنَّارِ يُفْتَنُونَ
ayahEnglish: [It is] the Day they will be tormented over the Fire.

targetTranslation: taste/experience! (command to a group) (referring to فِتْنَتَكُمْ - your trial)
ayahArabic: ذُوقُوا۟ فِتْنَتَكُمْ هٰذَا ٱلَّذِى كُنتُم بِهِۦ تَسْتَعْجِلُونَ
ayahEnglish: [And will be told], "Taste your torment. This is that for which you were impatient."

targetTranslation: your trial (referring to the punishment mentioned in ذُوقُوا)
ayahArabic: ذُوقُوا۟ فِتْنَتَكُمْ هٰذَا ٱلَّذِى كُنتُم بِهِۦ تَسْتَعْجِلُونَ
ayahEnglish: [And will be told], "Taste your torment. This is that for which you were impatient."

ayahArabic: ذُوقُوا۟ فِتْنَتَكُمْ هٰذَا ٱلَّذِى كُنتُم بِهِۦ تَسْتَعْجِلُونَ
ayahEnglish: [And will be told], "Taste your torment. This is that for which you were impatient."

ayahArabic: ذُوقُوا۟ فِتْنَتَكُمْ هٰذَا ٱلَّذِى كُنتُم بِهِۦ تَسْتَعْجِلُونَ
ayahEnglish: [And will be told], "Taste your torment. This is that for which you were impatient."

ayahArabic: ذُوقُوا۟ فِتْنَتَكُمْ هٰذَا ٱلَّذِى كُنتُم بِهِۦ تَسْتَعْجِلُونَ
ayahEnglish: [And will be told], "Taste your torment. This is that for which you were impatient."

ayahArabic: إِنَّ ٱلْمُتَّقِينَ فِى جَنَّاتٍ وَعُيُونٍ
ayahEnglish: Indeed, the righteous will be among gardens and springs,

targetTranslation: those who are taking (subject of كَانُوا)
ayahArabic: ءَاخِذِينَ مَآ ءَاتَىٰهُمْ رَبُّهُمْ إِنَّهُمْ كَانُوا۟ قَبْلَ ذٰلِكَ مُحْسِنِينَ
ayahEnglish: Accepting what their Lord has given them. Indeed, they were before that doers of good.

ayahArabic: ءَاخِذِينَ مَآ ءَاتَىٰهُمْ رَبُّهُمْ إِنَّهُمْ كَانُوا۟ قَبْلَ ذٰلِكَ مُحْسِنِينَ
ayahEnglish: Accepting what their Lord has given them. Indeed, they were before that doers of good.

ayahArabic: ءَاخِذِينَ مَآ ءَاتَىٰهُمْ رَبُّهُمْ إِنَّهُمْ كَانُوا۟ قَبْلَ ذٰلِكَ مُحْسِنِينَ
ayahEnglish: Accepting what their Lord has given them. Indeed, they were before that doers of good.

ayahArabic: ءَاخِذِينَ مَآ ءَاتَىٰهُمْ رَبُّهُمْ إِنَّهُمْ كَانُوا۟ قَبْلَ ذٰلِكَ مُحْسِنِينَ
ayahEnglish: Accepting what their Lord has given them. Indeed, they were before that doers of good.

targetTranslation: they were (describing مُحْسِنِينَ - those who do good)
ayahArabic: ءَاخِذِينَ مَآ ءَاتَىٰهُمْ رَبُّهُمْ إِنَّهُمْ كَانُوا۟ قَبْلَ ذٰلِكَ مُحْسِنِينَ
ayahEnglish: Accepting what their Lord has given them. Indeed, they were before that doers of good.

ayahArabic: ءَاخِذِينَ مَآ ءَاتَىٰهُمْ رَبُّهُمْ إِنَّهُمْ كَانُوا۟ قَبْلَ ذٰلِكَ مُحْسِنِينَ
ayahEnglish: Accepting what their Lord has given them. Indeed, they were before that doers of good.

targetTranslation: that (far) (points to what came before in the ayah)
ayahArabic: ءَاخِذِينَ مَآ ءَاتَىٰهُمْ رَبُّهُمْ إِنَّهُمْ كَانُوا۟ قَبْلَ ذٰلِكَ مُحْسِنِينَ
ayahEnglish: Accepting what their Lord has given them. Indeed, they were before that doers of good.

targetTranslation: those who do good (object of كَانُوا)
ayahArabic: ءَاخِذِينَ مَآ ءَاتَىٰهُمْ رَبُّهُمْ إِنَّهُمْ كَانُوا۟ قَبْلَ ذٰلِكَ مُحْسِنِينَ
ayahEnglish: Accepting what their Lord has given them. Indeed, they were before that doers of good.

ayahArabic: كَانُوا۟ قَلِيلًا مِّنَ ٱلَّيْلِ مَا يَهْجَعُونَ
ayahEnglish: They used to sleep but little of the night,

targetTranslation: a little (describing how much they slept)
ayahArabic: كَانُوا۟ قَلِيلًا مِّنَ ٱلَّيْلِ مَا يَهْجَعُونَ
ayahEnglish: They used to sleep but little of the night,

targetTranslation: that which (connects to يَهْجَعُونَ - they sleep)
ayahArabic: كَانُوا۟ قَلِيلًا مِّنَ ٱلَّيْلِ مَا يَهْجَعُونَ
ayahEnglish: They used to sleep but little of the night,

targetTranslation: they sleep lightly (describing the righteous people mentioned earlier in the ayah)
ayahArabic: كَانُوا۟ قَلِيلًا مِّنَ ٱلَّيْلِ مَا يَهْجَعُونَ
ayahEnglish: They used to sleep but little of the night,

ayahArabic: وَبِٱلْأَسْحَارِ هُمْ يَسْتَغْفِرُونَ
ayahEnglish: And in the hours before dawn they would ask forgiveness,

ayahArabic: وَبِٱلْأَسْحَارِ هُمْ يَسْتَغْفِرُونَ
ayahEnglish: And in the hours before dawn they would ask forgiveness,

targetTranslation: their wealth (in the phrase 'in their wealth')
ayahArabic: وَفِىٓ أَمْوٰلِهِمْ حَقٌّ لِّلسَّآئِلِ وَٱلْمَحْرُومِ
ayahEnglish: And from their properties was [given] the right of the [needy] petitioner and the deprived.

ayahArabic: وَفِىٓ أَمْوٰلِهِمْ حَقٌّ لِّلسَّآئِلِ وَٱلْمَحْرُومِ
ayahEnglish: And from their properties was [given] the right of the [needy] petitioner and the deprived.

ayahArabic: وَفِى ٱلْأَرْضِ ءَايٰتٌ لِّلْمُوقِنِينَ
ayahEnglish: And on the earth are signs for the certain [in faith]

targetTranslation: your selves (referring to those being addressed)
ayahArabic: وَفِىٓ أَنفُسِكُمْ أَفَلَا تُبْصِرُونَ
ayahEnglish: And in yourselves. Then will you not see?

targetTranslation: then will you not? (subject of تُبْصِرُونَ)
ayahArabic: وَفِىٓ أَنفُسِكُمْ أَفَلَا تُبْصِرُونَ
ayahEnglish: And in yourselves. Then will you not see?

ayahArabic: وَفِىٓ أَنفُسِكُمْ أَفَلَا تُبْصِرُونَ
ayahEnglish: And in yourselves. Then will you not see?

targetTranslation: your (all of you) provision (in السَّمَآءِ/the sky)
ayahArabic: وَفِى ٱلسَّمَآءِ رِزْقُكُمْ وَمَا تُوعَدُونَ
ayahEnglish: And in the heaven is your provision and whatever you are promised.

ayahArabic: وَفِى ٱلسَّمَآءِ رِزْقُكُمْ وَمَا تُوعَدُونَ
ayahEnglish: And in the heaven is your provision and whatever you are promised.

ayahArabic: فَوَرَبِّ ٱلسَّمَآءِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ إِنَّهُۥ لَحَقٌّ مِّثْلَ مَآ أَنَّكُمْ تَنطِقُونَ
ayahEnglish: Then by the Lord of the heaven and earth, indeed, it is truth - just as [sure as] it is that you are speaking.

ayahArabic: فَوَرَبِّ ٱلسَّمَآءِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ إِنَّهُۥ لَحَقٌّ مِّثْلَ مَآ أَنَّكُمْ تَنطِقُونَ
ayahEnglish: Then by the Lord of the heaven and earth, indeed, it is truth - just as [sure as] it is that you are speaking.

ayahArabic: فَوَرَبِّ ٱلسَّمَآءِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ إِنَّهُۥ لَحَقٌّ مِّثْلَ مَآ أَنَّكُمْ تَنطِقُونَ
ayahEnglish: Then by the Lord of the heaven and earth, indeed, it is truth - just as [sure as] it is that you are speaking.

targetTranslation: he came to you (object of هَلْ - 'has')
ayahArabic: هَلْ أَتَىٰكَ حَدِيثُ ضَيْفِ إِبْرٰهِيمَ ٱلْمُكْرَمِينَ
ayahEnglish: Has there reached you the story of the honored guests of Abraham? -

ayahArabic: هَلْ أَتَىٰكَ حَدِيثُ ضَيْفِ إِبْرٰهِيمَ ٱلْمُكْرَمِينَ
ayahEnglish: Has there reached you the story of the honored guests of Abraham? -

ayahArabic: هَلْ أَتَىٰكَ حَدِيثُ ضَيْفِ إِبْرٰهِيمَ ٱلْمُكْرَمِينَ
ayahEnglish: Has there reached you the story of the honored guests of Abraham? -

ayahArabic: هَلْ أَتَىٰكَ حَدِيثُ ضَيْفِ إِبْرٰهِيمَ ٱلْمُكْرَمِينَ
ayahEnglish: Has there reached you the story of the honored guests of Abraham? -

ayahArabic: إِذْ دَخَلُوا۟ عَلَيْهِ فَقَالُوا۟ سَلٰمًا قَالَ سَلٰمٌ قَوْمٌ مُّنكَرُونَ
ayahEnglish: When they entered upon him and said, "[We greet you with] peace." He answered, "[And upon you] peace; [you are] a people unknown."

ayahArabic: إِذْ دَخَلُوا۟ عَلَيْهِ فَقَالُوا۟ سَلٰمًا قَالَ سَلٰمٌ قَوْمٌ مُّنكَرُونَ
ayahEnglish: When they entered upon him and said, "[We greet you with] peace." He answered, "[And upon you] peace; [you are] a people unknown."

ayahArabic: إِذْ دَخَلُوا۟ عَلَيْهِ فَقَالُوا۟ سَلٰمًا قَالَ سَلٰمٌ قَوْمٌ مُّنكَرُونَ
ayahEnglish: When they entered upon him and said, "[We greet you with] peace." He answered, "[And upon you] peace; [you are] a people unknown."

ayahArabic: إِذْ دَخَلُوا۟ عَلَيْهِ فَقَالُوا۟ سَلٰمًا قَالَ سَلٰمٌ قَوْمٌ مُّنكَرُونَ
ayahEnglish: When they entered upon him and said, "[We greet you with] peace." He answered, "[And upon you] peace; [you are] a people unknown."

ayahArabic: إِذْ دَخَلُوا۟ عَلَيْهِ فَقَالُوا۟ سَلٰمًا قَالَ سَلٰمٌ قَوْمٌ مُّنكَرُونَ
ayahEnglish: When they entered upon him and said, "[We greet you with] peace." He answered, "[And upon you] peace; [you are] a people unknown."

ayahArabic: إِذْ دَخَلُوا۟ عَلَيْهِ فَقَالُوا۟ سَلٰمًا قَالَ سَلٰمٌ قَوْمٌ مُّنكَرُونَ
ayahEnglish: When they entered upon him and said, "[We greet you with] peace." He answered, "[And upon you] peace; [you are] a people unknown."

targetTranslation: unknown ones, strangers (describing the angels who visited Ibrahim)
ayahArabic: إِذْ دَخَلُوا۟ عَلَيْهِ فَقَالُوا۟ سَلٰمًا قَالَ سَلٰمٌ قَوْمٌ مُّنكَرُونَ
ayahEnglish: When they entered upon him and said, "[We greet you with] peace." He answered, "[And upon you] peace; [you are] a people unknown."

ayahArabic: فَرَاغَ إِلَىٰٓ أَهْلِهِۦ فَجَآءَ بِعِجْلٍ سَمِينٍ
ayahEnglish: Then he went to his family and came with a fat [roasted] calf.

ayahArabic: فَرَاغَ إِلَىٰٓ أَهْلِهِۦ فَجَآءَ بِعِجْلٍ سَمِينٍ
ayahEnglish: Then he went to his family and came with a fat [roasted] calf.

targetTranslation: he said (introducing what he offered to his guests)
ayahArabic: فَقَرَّبَهُۥٓ إِلَيْهِمْ قَالَ أَلَا تَأْكُلُونَ
ayahEnglish: And placed it near them; he said, "Will you not eat?"

targetTranslation: will you not? (asking about تَأْكُلُونَ - eating)
ayahArabic: فَقَرَّبَهُۥٓ إِلَيْهِمْ قَالَ أَلَا تَأْكُلُونَ
ayahEnglish: And placed it near them; he said, "Will you not eat?"

ayahArabic: فَقَرَّبَهُۥٓ إِلَيْهِمْ قَالَ أَلَا تَأْكُلُونَ
ayahEnglish: And placed it near them; he said, "Will you not eat?"

targetTranslation: a fear (object of أَوْجَسَ - he felt)
ayahArabic: فَأَوْجَسَ مِنْهُمْ خِيفَةً قَالُوا۟ لَا تَخَفْ وَبَشَّرُوهُ بِغُلٰمٍ عَلِيمٍ
ayahEnglish: And he felt from them apprehension. They said, "Fear not," and gave him good tidings of a learned boy.

targetTranslation: they said (to him when he was afraid of them)
ayahArabic: فَأَوْجَسَ مِنْهُمْ خِيفَةً قَالُوا۟ لَا تَخَفْ وَبَشَّرُوهُ بِغُلٰمٍ عَلِيمٍ
ayahEnglish: And he felt from them apprehension. They said, "Fear not," and gave him good tidings of a learned boy.

ayahArabic: فَأَوْجَسَ مِنْهُمْ خِيفَةً قَالُوا۟ لَا تَخَفْ وَبَشَّرُوهُ بِغُلٰمٍ عَلِيمٍ
ayahEnglish: And he felt from them apprehension. They said, "Fear not," and gave him good tidings of a learned boy.

ayahArabic: فَأَوْجَسَ مِنْهُمْ خِيفَةً قَالُوا۟ لَا تَخَفْ وَبَشَّرُوهُ بِغُلٰمٍ عَلِيمٍ
ayahEnglish: And he felt from them apprehension. They said, "Fear not," and gave him good tidings of a learned boy.

ayahArabic: فَأَقْبَلَتِ ٱمْرَأَتُهُۥ فِى صَرَّةٍ فَصَكَّتْ وَجْهَهَا وَقَالَتْ عَجُوزٌ عَقِيمٌ
ayahEnglish: And his wife approached with a cry [of alarm] and struck her face and said, "[I am] a barren old woman!"

targetTranslation: with a loud cry (describing how امْرَأَتُهُ came)
ayahArabic: فَأَقْبَلَتِ ٱمْرَأَتُهُۥ فِى صَرَّةٍ فَصَكَّتْ وَجْهَهَا وَقَالَتْ عَجُوزٌ عَقِيمٌ
ayahEnglish: And his wife approached with a cry [of alarm] and struck her face and said, "[I am] a barren old woman!"

targetTranslation: her face (acted upon by فَصَكَّتْ - she struck)
ayahArabic: فَأَقْبَلَتِ ٱمْرَأَتُهُۥ فِى صَرَّةٍ فَصَكَّتْ وَجْهَهَا وَقَالَتْ عَجُوزٌ عَقِيمٌ
ayahEnglish: And his wife approached with a cry [of alarm] and struck her face and said, "[I am] a barren old woman!"

ayahArabic: فَأَقْبَلَتِ ٱمْرَأَتُهُۥ فِى صَرَّةٍ فَصَكَّتْ وَجْهَهَا وَقَالَتْ عَجُوزٌ عَقِيمٌ
ayahEnglish: And his wife approached with a cry [of alarm] and struck her face and said, "[I am] a barren old woman!"

ayahArabic: فَأَقْبَلَتِ ٱمْرَأَتُهُۥ فِى صَرَّةٍ فَصَكَّتْ وَجْهَهَا وَقَالَتْ عَجُوزٌ عَقِيمٌ
ayahEnglish: And his wife approached with a cry [of alarm] and struck her face and said, "[I am] a barren old woman!"

ayahArabic: قَالُوا۟ كَذٰلِكِ قَالَ رَبُّكِ إِنَّهُۥ هُوَ ٱلْحَكِيمُ ٱلْعَلِيمُ
ayahEnglish: They said, "Thus has said your Lord; indeed, He is the Wise, the Knowing."

ayahArabic: قَالُوا۟ كَذٰلِكِ قَالَ رَبُّكِ إِنَّهُۥ هُوَ ٱلْحَكِيمُ ٱلْعَلِيمُ
ayahEnglish: They said, "Thus has said your Lord; indeed, He is the Wise, the Knowing."

targetTranslation: your Lord (feminine, as the doer of قَالَ - said)
ayahArabic: قَالُوا۟ كَذٰلِكِ قَالَ رَبُّكِ إِنَّهُۥ هُوَ ٱلْحَكِيمُ ٱلْعَلِيمُ
ayahEnglish: They said, "Thus has said your Lord; indeed, He is the Wise, the Knowing."

targetTranslation: He (referring to Allah in this ayah)
ayahArabic: قَالُوا۟ كَذٰلِكِ قَالَ رَبُّكِ إِنَّهُۥ هُوَ ٱلْحَكِيمُ ٱلْعَلِيمُ
ayahEnglish: They said, "Thus has said your Lord; indeed, He is the Wise, the Knowing."

ayahArabic: قَالَ فَمَا خَطْبُكُمْ أَيُّهَا ٱلْمُرْسَلُونَ
ayahEnglish: [Abraham] said, "Then what is your business [here], O messengers?"

ayahArabic: قَالَ فَمَا خَطْبُكُمْ أَيُّهَا ٱلْمُرْسَلُونَ
ayahEnglish: [Abraham] said, "Then what is your business [here], O messengers?"

ayahArabic: قَالَ فَمَا خَطْبُكُمْ أَيُّهَا ٱلْمُرْسَلُونَ
ayahEnglish: [Abraham] said, "Then what is your business [here], O messengers?"

ayahArabic: قَالُوٓا۟ إِنَّآ أُرْسِلْنَآ إِلَىٰ قَوْمٍ مُّجْرِمِينَ
ayahEnglish: They said, "Indeed, we have been sent to a people of criminals

ayahArabic: قَالُوٓا۟ إِنَّآ أُرْسِلْنَآ إِلَىٰ قَوْمٍ مُّجْرِمِينَ
ayahEnglish: They said, "Indeed, we have been sent to a people of criminals

targetTranslation: of a people/tribe (possessed by مُّجْرِمِينَ - criminals)
ayahArabic: قَالُوٓا۟ إِنَّآ أُرْسِلْنَآ إِلَىٰ قَوْمٍ مُّجْرِمِينَ
ayahEnglish: They said, "Indeed, we have been sent to a people of criminals

ayahArabic: قَالُوٓا۟ إِنَّآ أُرْسِلْنَآ إِلَىٰ قَوْمٍ مُّجْرِمِينَ
ayahEnglish: They said, "Indeed, we have been sent to a people of criminals

ayahArabic: لِنُرْسِلَ عَلَيْهِمْ حِجَارَةً مِّن طِينٍ
ayahEnglish: To send down upon them stones of clay,

ayahArabic: لِنُرْسِلَ عَلَيْهِمْ حِجَارَةً مِّن طِينٍ
ayahEnglish: To send down upon them stones of clay,

ayahArabic: مُّسَوَّمَةً عِندَ رَبِّكَ لِلْمُسْرِفِينَ
ayahEnglish: Marked in the presence of your Lord for the transgressors."

ayahArabic: مُّسَوَّمَةً عِندَ رَبِّكَ لِلْمُسْرِفِينَ
ayahEnglish: Marked in the presence of your Lord for the transgressors."

ayahArabic: مُّسَوَّمَةً عِندَ رَبِّكَ لِلْمُسْرِفِينَ
ayahEnglish: Marked in the presence of your Lord for the transgressors."

ayahArabic: فَأَخْرَجْنَا مَن كَانَ فِيهَا مِنَ ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ
ayahEnglish: So We brought out whoever was in them [i.e., the cities] of the believers.

ayahArabic: فَأَخْرَجْنَا مَن كَانَ فِيهَا مِنَ ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ
ayahEnglish: So We brought out whoever was in them [i.e., the cities] of the believers.

ayahArabic: فَمَا وَجَدْنَا فِيهَا غَيْرَ بَيْتٍ مِّنَ ٱلْمُسْلِمِينَ
ayahEnglish: And We found not within them other than a [single] house of Muslims.

ayahArabic: فَمَا وَجَدْنَا فِيهَا غَيْرَ بَيْتٍ مِّنَ ٱلْمُسْلِمِينَ
ayahEnglish: And We found not within them other than a [single] house of Muslims.

ayahArabic: فَمَا وَجَدْنَا فِيهَا غَيْرَ بَيْتٍ مِّنَ ٱلْمُسْلِمِينَ
ayahEnglish: And We found not within them other than a [single] house of Muslims.

targetTranslation: a sign/miracle (object of تَرَكْنَا - we left)
ayahArabic: وَتَرَكْنَا فِيهَآ ءَايَةً لِّلَّذِينَ يَخَافُونَ ٱلْعَذَابَ ٱلْأَلِيمَ
ayahEnglish: And We left therein a sign for those who fear the painful punishment.

targetTranslation: they fear (those who fear الْعَذَابَ الْأَلِيمَ - the painful punishment)
ayahArabic: وَتَرَكْنَا فِيهَآ ءَايَةً لِّلَّذِينَ يَخَافُونَ ٱلْعَذَابَ ٱلْأَلِيمَ
ayahEnglish: And We left therein a sign for those who fear the painful punishment.

targetTranslation: Moses (subject of أَرْسَلْنٰهُ - we sent him)
ayahArabic: وَفِى مُوسَىٰٓ إِذْ أَرْسَلْنٰهُ إِلَىٰ فِرْعَوْنَ بِسُلْطٰنٍ مُّبِينٍ
ayahEnglish: And in Moses [was a sign], when We sent him to Pharaoh with clear authority.

targetTranslation: when (connecting to أَرْسَلْنٰهُ - we sent him)
ayahArabic: وَفِى مُوسَىٰٓ إِذْ أَرْسَلْنٰهُ إِلَىٰ فِرْعَوْنَ بِسُلْطٰنٍ مُّبِينٍ
ayahEnglish: And in Moses [was a sign], when We sent him to Pharaoh with clear authority.

ayahArabic: وَفِى مُوسَىٰٓ إِذْ أَرْسَلْنٰهُ إِلَىٰ فِرْعَوْنَ بِسُلْطٰنٍ مُّبِينٍ
ayahEnglish: And in Moses [was a sign], when We sent him to Pharaoh with clear authority.

targetTranslation: Pharaoh (object of أَرْسَلْنٰهُ - we sent him)
ayahArabic: وَفِى مُوسَىٰٓ إِذْ أَرْسَلْنٰهُ إِلَىٰ فِرْعَوْنَ بِسُلْطٰنٍ مُّبِينٍ
ayahEnglish: And in Moses [was a sign], when We sent him to Pharaoh with clear authority.

ayahArabic: وَفِى مُوسَىٰٓ إِذْ أَرْسَلْنٰهُ إِلَىٰ فِرْعَوْنَ بِسُلْطٰنٍ مُّبِينٍ
ayahEnglish: And in Moses [was a sign], when We sent him to Pharaoh with clear authority.

ayahArabic: فَتَوَلَّىٰ بِرُكْنِهِۦ وَقَالَ سٰحِرٌ أَوْ مَجْنُونٌ
ayahEnglish: But he turned away with his supporters and said, "A magician or a madman."

ayahArabic: فَتَوَلَّىٰ بِرُكْنِهِۦ وَقَالَ سٰحِرٌ أَوْ مَجْنُونٌ
ayahEnglish: But he turned away with his supporters and said, "A magician or a madman."

ayahArabic: فَأَخَذْنٰهُ وَجُنُودَهُۥ فَنَبَذْنٰهُمْ فِى ٱلْيَمِّ وَهُوَ مُلِيمٌ
ayahEnglish: So We took him and his soldiers and cast them into the sea, and he was blameworthy.

ayahArabic: وَفِى عَادٍ إِذْ أَرْسَلْنَا عَلَيْهِمُ ٱلرِّيحَ ٱلْعَقِيمَ
ayahEnglish: And in ʿAad [was a sign], when We sent against them the barren wind.

ayahArabic: وَفِى عَادٍ إِذْ أَرْسَلْنَا عَلَيْهِمُ ٱلرِّيحَ ٱلْعَقِيمَ
ayahEnglish: And in ʿAad [was a sign], when We sent against them the barren wind.

ayahArabic: وَفِى عَادٍ إِذْ أَرْسَلْنَا عَلَيْهِمُ ٱلرِّيحَ ٱلْعَقِيمَ
ayahEnglish: And in ʿAad [was a sign], when We sent against them the barren wind.

targetTranslation: she leaves (subject of wind in أَتَتْ)
ayahArabic: مَا تَذَرُ مِن شَىْءٍ أَتَتْ عَلَيْهِ إِلَّا جَعَلَتْهُ كَٱلرَّمِيمِ
ayahEnglish: It left nothing of what it came upon but that it made it like disintegrated ruins.

targetTranslation: a thing (that is acted upon by عَلَيْهِ)
ayahArabic: مَا تَذَرُ مِن شَىْءٍ أَتَتْ عَلَيْهِ إِلَّا جَعَلَتْهُ كَٱلرَّمِيمِ
ayahEnglish: It left nothing of what it came upon but that it made it like disintegrated ruins.

targetTranslation: she came (subject of wind in تَذَرُ)
ayahArabic: مَا تَذَرُ مِن شَىْءٍ أَتَتْ عَلَيْهِ إِلَّا جَعَلَتْهُ كَٱلرَّمِيمِ
ayahEnglish: It left nothing of what it came upon but that it made it like disintegrated ruins.

targetTranslation: she made it (object of تَذَرُ - she leaves)
ayahArabic: مَا تَذَرُ مِن شَىْءٍ أَتَتْ عَلَيْهِ إِلَّا جَعَلَتْهُ كَٱلرَّمِيمِ
ayahEnglish: It left nothing of what it came upon but that it made it like disintegrated ruins.

targetTranslation: Thamud (as the object being referred to)
ayahArabic: وَفِى ثَمُودَ إِذْ قِيلَ لَهُمْ تَمَتَّعُوا۟ حَتَّىٰ حِينٍ
ayahEnglish: And in Thamūd, when it was said to them, "Enjoy yourselves for a time."

targetTranslation: when (referring to قِيلَ - it was said)
ayahArabic: وَفِى ثَمُودَ إِذْ قِيلَ لَهُمْ تَمَتَّعُوا۟ حَتَّىٰ حِينٍ
ayahEnglish: And in Thamūd, when it was said to them, "Enjoy yourselves for a time."

ayahArabic: وَفِى ثَمُودَ إِذْ قِيلَ لَهُمْ تَمَتَّعُوا۟ حَتَّىٰ حِينٍ
ayahEnglish: And in Thamūd, when it was said to them, "Enjoy yourselves for a time."

ayahArabic: وَفِى ثَمُودَ إِذْ قِيلَ لَهُمْ تَمَتَّعُوا۟ حَتَّىٰ حِينٍ
ayahEnglish: And in Thamūd, when it was said to them, "Enjoy yourselves for a time."

ayahArabic: وَفِى ثَمُودَ إِذْ قِيلَ لَهُمْ تَمَتَّعُوا۟ حَتَّىٰ حِينٍ
ayahEnglish: And in Thamūd, when it was said to them, "Enjoy yourselves for a time."

targetTranslation: command of (possessed by رَبِّهِمْ - their Lord)
ayahArabic: فَعَتَوْا۟ عَنْ أَمْرِ رَبِّهِمْ فَأَخَذَتْهُمُ ٱلصَّاعِقَةُ وَهُمْ يَنظُرُونَ
ayahEnglish: But they were insolent toward the command of their Lord, so the thunderbolt seized them while they were looking on.

ayahArabic: فَعَتَوْا۟ عَنْ أَمْرِ رَبِّهِمْ فَأَخَذَتْهُمُ ٱلصَّاعِقَةُ وَهُمْ يَنظُرُونَ
ayahEnglish: But they were insolent toward the command of their Lord, so the thunderbolt seized them while they were looking on.

targetTranslation: they look/observe (while the punishment struck them)
ayahArabic: فَعَتَوْا۟ عَنْ أَمْرِ رَبِّهِمْ فَأَخَذَتْهُمُ ٱلصَّاعِقَةُ وَهُمْ يَنظُرُونَ
ayahEnglish: But they were insolent toward the command of their Lord, so the thunderbolt seized them while they were looking on.

ayahArabic: فَمَا ٱسْتَطٰعُوا۟ مِن قِيَامٍ وَمَا كَانُوا۟ مُنتَصِرِينَ
ayahEnglish: And they were unable to arise, nor could they defend themselves.

ayahArabic: فَمَا ٱسْتَطٰعُوا۟ مِن قِيَامٍ وَمَا كَانُوا۟ مُنتَصِرِينَ
ayahEnglish: And they were unable to arise, nor could they defend themselves.

ayahArabic: فَمَا ٱسْتَطٰعُوا۟ مِن قِيَامٍ وَمَا كَانُوا۟ مُنتَصِرِينَ
ayahEnglish: And they were unable to arise, nor could they defend themselves.

targetTranslation: ones who become victorious (predicate of كَانُوا)
ayahArabic: فَمَا ٱسْتَطٰعُوا۟ مِن قِيَامٍ وَمَا كَانُوا۟ مُنتَصِرِينَ
ayahEnglish: And they were unable to arise, nor could they defend themselves.

targetTranslation: Noah's (proper name, being described by قَوْمَ)
ayahArabic: وَقَوْمَ نُوحٍ مِّن قَبْلُ إِنَّهُمْ كَانُوا۟ قَوْمًا فٰسِقِينَ
ayahEnglish: And [We destroyed] the people of Noah before; indeed, they were a people defiantly disobedient.

ayahArabic: وَقَوْمَ نُوحٍ مِّن قَبْلُ إِنَّهُمْ كَانُوا۟ قَوْمًا فٰسِقِينَ
ayahEnglish: And [We destroyed] the people of Noah before; indeed, they were a people defiantly disobedient.

targetTranslation: they were (referring to قَوْمَ نُوحٍ - the people of Noah)
ayahArabic: وَقَوْمَ نُوحٍ مِّن قَبْلُ إِنَّهُمْ كَانُوا۟ قَوْمًا فٰسِقِينَ
ayahEnglish: And [We destroyed] the people of Noah before; indeed, they were a people defiantly disobedient.

ayahArabic: وَقَوْمَ نُوحٍ مِّن قَبْلُ إِنَّهُمْ كَانُوا۟ قَوْمًا فٰسِقِينَ
ayahEnglish: And [We destroyed] the people of Noah before; indeed, they were a people defiantly disobedient.

ayahArabic: وَقَوْمَ نُوحٍ مِّن قَبْلُ إِنَّهُمْ كَانُوا۟ قَوْمًا فٰسِقِينَ
ayahEnglish: And [We destroyed] the people of Noah before; indeed, they were a people defiantly disobedient.

targetTranslation: we built it (referring to السَّمَاء/the heaven)
ayahArabic: وَٱلسَّمَآءَ بَنَيْنٰهَا بِأَيْي۟دٍ وَإِنَّا لَمُوسِعُونَ
ayahEnglish: And the heaven We constructed with strength, and indeed, We are [its] expander.

ayahArabic: وَٱلْأَرْضَ فَرَشْنٰهَا فَنِعْمَ ٱلْمٰهِدُونَ
ayahEnglish: And the earth We have spread out, and excellent is the preparer.

ayahArabic: وَمِن كُلِّ شَىْءٍ خَلَقْنَا زَوْجَيْنِ لَعَلَّكُمْ تَذَكَّرُونَ
ayahEnglish: And of all things We created two mates [i.e., counterparts]; perhaps you will remember.

ayahArabic: وَمِن كُلِّ شَىْءٍ خَلَقْنَا زَوْجَيْنِ لَعَلَّكُمْ تَذَكَّرُونَ
ayahEnglish: And of all things We created two mates [i.e., counterparts]; perhaps you will remember.

targetTranslation: We created (referring to Allah using the royal 'We')
ayahArabic: وَمِن كُلِّ شَىْءٍ خَلَقْنَا زَوْجَيْنِ لَعَلَّكُمْ تَذَكَّرُونَ
ayahEnglish: And of all things We created two mates [i.e., counterparts]; perhaps you will remember.

ayahArabic: وَمِن كُلِّ شَىْءٍ خَلَقْنَا زَوْجَيْنِ لَعَلَّكُمْ تَذَكَّرُونَ
ayahEnglish: And of all things We created two mates [i.e., counterparts]; perhaps you will remember.

ayahArabic: وَمِن كُلِّ شَىْءٍ خَلَقْنَا زَوْجَيْنِ لَعَلَّكُمْ تَذَكَّرُونَ
ayahEnglish: And of all things We created two mates [i.e., counterparts]; perhaps you will remember.

ayahArabic: فَفِرُّوٓا۟ إِلَى ٱللَّهِ إِنِّى لَكُم مِّنْهُ نَذِيرٌ مُّبِينٌ
ayahEnglish: So flee to Allāh. Indeed, I am to you from Him a clear warner.

targetTranslation: a warner (describing the speaker's role in relation to إِنِّى)
ayahArabic: فَفِرُّوٓا۟ إِلَى ٱللَّهِ إِنِّى لَكُم مِّنْهُ نَذِيرٌ مُّبِينٌ
ayahEnglish: So flee to Allāh. Indeed, I am to you from Him a clear warner.

targetTranslation: clear, evident (describing something in this ayah)
ayahArabic: فَفِرُّوٓا۟ إِلَى ٱللَّهِ إِنِّى لَكُم مِّنْهُ نَذِيرٌ مُّبِينٌ
ayahEnglish: So flee to Allāh. Indeed, I am to you from Him a clear warner.

targetTranslation: you (plural) make/set/place (in relation to اللَّهِ - Allah)
ayahArabic: وَلَا تَجْعَلُوا۟ مَعَ ٱللَّهِ إِلٰهًا ءَاخَرَ إِنِّى لَكُم مِّنْهُ نَذِيرٌ مُّبِينٌ
ayahEnglish: And do not make [as equal] with Allāh another deity. Indeed, I am to you from Him a clear warner.

targetTranslation: with/along with (connects اللَّهِ to previous phrase)
ayahArabic: وَلَا تَجْعَلُوا۟ مَعَ ٱللَّهِ إِلٰهًا ءَاخَرَ إِنِّى لَكُم مِّنْهُ نَذِيرٌ مُّبِينٌ
ayahEnglish: And do not make [as equal] with Allāh another deity. Indeed, I am to you from Him a clear warner.

ayahArabic: وَلَا تَجْعَلُوا۟ مَعَ ٱللَّهِ إِلٰهًا ءَاخَرَ إِنِّى لَكُم مِّنْهُ نَذِيرٌ مُّبِينٌ
ayahEnglish: And do not make [as equal] with Allāh another deity. Indeed, I am to you from Him a clear warner.

ayahArabic: وَلَا تَجْعَلُوا۟ مَعَ ٱللَّهِ إِلٰهًا ءَاخَرَ إِنِّى لَكُم مِّنْهُ نَذِيرٌ مُّبِينٌ
ayahEnglish: And do not make [as equal] with Allāh another deity. Indeed, I am to you from Him a clear warner.

ayahArabic: وَلَا تَجْعَلُوا۟ مَعَ ٱللَّهِ إِلٰهًا ءَاخَرَ إِنِّى لَكُم مِّنْهُ نَذِيرٌ مُّبِينٌ
ayahEnglish: And do not make [as equal] with Allāh another deity. Indeed, I am to you from Him a clear warner.

targetTranslation: a warner (one who warns others about Allah's punishment)
ayahArabic: وَلَا تَجْعَلُوا۟ مَعَ ٱللَّهِ إِلٰهًا ءَاخَرَ إِنِّى لَكُم مِّنْهُ نَذِيرٌ مُّبِينٌ
ayahEnglish: And do not make [as equal] with Allāh another deity. Indeed, I am to you from Him a clear warner.

ayahArabic: وَلَا تَجْعَلُوا۟ مَعَ ٱللَّهِ إِلٰهًا ءَاخَرَ إِنِّى لَكُم مِّنْهُ نَذِيرٌ مُّبِينٌ
ayahEnglish: And do not make [as equal] with Allāh another deity. Indeed, I am to you from Him a clear warner.

ayahArabic: كَذٰلِكَ مَآ أَتَى ٱلَّذِينَ مِن قَبْلِهِم مِّن رَّسُولٍ إِلَّا قَالُوا۟ سَاحِرٌ أَوْ مَجْنُونٌ
ayahEnglish: Similarly, there came not to those before them any messenger except that they said, "A magician or a madman."

ayahArabic: كَذٰلِكَ مَآ أَتَى ٱلَّذِينَ مِن قَبْلِهِم مِّن رَّسُولٍ إِلَّا قَالُوا۟ سَاحِرٌ أَوْ مَجْنُونٌ
ayahEnglish: Similarly, there came not to those before them any messenger except that they said, "A magician or a madman."

targetTranslation: before them (referring to those who came before)
ayahArabic: كَذٰلِكَ مَآ أَتَى ٱلَّذِينَ مِن قَبْلِهِم مِّن رَّسُولٍ إِلَّا قَالُوا۟ سَاحِرٌ أَوْ مَجْنُونٌ
ayahEnglish: Similarly, there came not to those before them any messenger except that they said, "A magician or a madman."

ayahArabic: كَذٰلِكَ مَآ أَتَى ٱلَّذِينَ مِن قَبْلِهِم مِّن رَّسُولٍ إِلَّا قَالُوا۟ سَاحِرٌ أَوْ مَجْنُونٌ
ayahEnglish: Similarly, there came not to those before them any messenger except that they said, "A magician or a madman."

ayahArabic: كَذٰلِكَ مَآ أَتَى ٱلَّذِينَ مِن قَبْلِهِم مِّن رَّسُولٍ إِلَّا قَالُوا۟ سَاحِرٌ أَوْ مَجْنُونٌ
ayahEnglish: Similarly, there came not to those before them any messenger except that they said, "A magician or a madman."

ayahArabic: كَذٰلِكَ مَآ أَتَى ٱلَّذِينَ مِن قَبْلِهِم مِّن رَّسُولٍ إِلَّا قَالُوا۟ سَاحِرٌ أَوْ مَجْنُونٌ
ayahEnglish: Similarly, there came not to those before them any messenger except that they said, "A magician or a madman."

targetTranslation: a madman/one possessed (subject of سَاحِرٌ أَوْ)
ayahArabic: كَذٰلِكَ مَآ أَتَى ٱلَّذِينَ مِن قَبْلِهِم مِّن رَّسُولٍ إِلَّا قَالُوا۟ سَاحِرٌ أَوْ مَجْنُونٌ
ayahEnglish: Similarly, there came not to those before them any messenger except that they said, "A magician or a madman."

ayahArabic: أَتَوَاصَوْا۟ بِهِۦ بَلْ هُمْ قَوْمٌ طَاغُونَ
ayahEnglish: Did they suggest it to them? Rather, they [themselves] are a transgressing people.

ayahArabic: أَتَوَاصَوْا۟ بِهِۦ بَلْ هُمْ قَوْمٌ طَاغُونَ
ayahEnglish: Did they suggest it to them? Rather, they [themselves] are a transgressing people.

ayahArabic: أَتَوَاصَوْا۟ بِهِۦ بَلْ هُمْ قَوْمٌ طَاغُونَ
ayahEnglish: Did they suggest it to them? Rather, they [themselves] are a transgressing people.

ayahArabic: أَتَوَاصَوْا۟ بِهِۦ بَلْ هُمْ قَوْمٌ طَاغُونَ
ayahEnglish: Did they suggest it to them? Rather, they [themselves] are a transgressing people.

targetTranslation: you (subject of فَمَآ...بِمَلُومٍ - 'then you are not blamed')
ayahArabic: فَتَوَلَّ عَنْهُمْ فَمَآ أَنتَ بِمَلُومٍ
ayahEnglish: So leave them, [O Muḥammad], for you are not to be blamed.

ayahArabic: وَذَكِّرْ فَإِنَّ ٱلذِّكْرَىٰ تَنفَعُ ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ
ayahEnglish: And remind, for indeed, the reminder benefits the believers.

ayahArabic: وَمَا خَلَقْتُ ٱلْجِنَّ وَٱلْإِنسَ إِلَّا لِيَعْبُدُونِ
ayahEnglish: And I did not create the jinn and mankind except to worship Me.

targetTranslation: I want (from مَآ أُرِيدُ - I do not want)
ayahArabic: مَآ أُرِيدُ مِنْهُم مِّن رِّزْقٍ وَمَآ أُرِيدُ أَن يُطْعِمُونِ
ayahEnglish: I do not want from them any provision, nor do I want them to feed Me.

ayahArabic: مَآ أُرِيدُ مِنْهُم مِّن رِّزْقٍ وَمَآ أُرِيدُ أَن يُطْعِمُونِ
ayahEnglish: I do not want from them any provision, nor do I want them to feed Me.

targetTranslation: I want (subject of the verb phrase مَآ أُرِيدُ - I do not want)
ayahArabic: مَآ أُرِيدُ مِنْهُم مِّن رِّزْقٍ وَمَآ أُرِيدُ أَن يُطْعِمُونِ
ayahEnglish: I do not want from them any provision, nor do I want them to feed Me.

ayahArabic: مَآ أُرِيدُ مِنْهُم مِّن رِّزْقٍ وَمَآ أُرِيدُ أَن يُطْعِمُونِ
ayahEnglish: I do not want from them any provision, nor do I want them to feed Me.

ayahArabic: إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ هُوَ ٱلرَّزَّاقُ ذُو ٱلْقُوَّةِ ٱلْمَتِينُ
ayahEnglish: Indeed, it is Allāh who is the [continual] Provider, the firm possessor of strength.

ayahArabic: إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ هُوَ ٱلرَّزَّاقُ ذُو ٱلْقُوَّةِ ٱلْمَتِينُ
ayahEnglish: Indeed, it is Allāh who is the [continual] Provider, the firm possessor of strength.

ayahArabic: إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ هُوَ ٱلرَّزَّاقُ ذُو ٱلْقُوَّةِ ٱلْمَتِينُ
ayahEnglish: Indeed, it is Allāh who is the [continual] Provider, the firm possessor of strength.

targetTranslation: they were unfair (to others) (subject of يَسْتَعْجِلُونِ)
ayahArabic: فَإِنَّ لِلَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا۟ ذَنُوبًا مِّثْلَ ذَنُوبِ أَصْحٰبِهِمْ فَلَا يَسْتَعْجِلُونِ
ayahEnglish: And indeed, for those who have wronged is a portion [of punishment] like the portion of their companions [i.e., predecessors], so let them not impatiently urge Me.

ayahArabic: فَإِنَّ لِلَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا۟ ذَنُوبًا مِّثْلَ ذَنُوبِ أَصْحٰبِهِمْ فَلَا يَسْتَعْجِلُونِ
ayahEnglish: And indeed, for those who have wronged is a portion [of punishment] like the portion of their companions [i.e., predecessors], so let them not impatiently urge Me.

ayahArabic: فَإِنَّ لِلَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا۟ ذَنُوبًا مِّثْلَ ذَنُوبِ أَصْحٰبِهِمْ فَلَا يَسْتَعْجِلُونِ
ayahEnglish: And indeed, for those who have wronged is a portion [of punishment] like the portion of their companions [i.e., predecessors], so let them not impatiently urge Me.

targetTranslation: of the big mistake (belonging to أَصْحٰبِ - companions)
ayahArabic: فَإِنَّ لِلَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا۟ ذَنُوبًا مِّثْلَ ذَنُوبِ أَصْحٰبِهِمْ فَلَا يَسْتَعْجِلُونِ
ayahEnglish: And indeed, for those who have wronged is a portion [of punishment] like the portion of their companions [i.e., predecessors], so let them not impatiently urge Me.

ayahArabic: فَإِنَّ لِلَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا۟ ذَنُوبًا مِّثْلَ ذَنُوبِ أَصْحٰبِهِمْ فَلَا يَسْتَعْجِلُونِ
ayahEnglish: And indeed, for those who have wronged is a portion [of punishment] like the portion of their companions [i.e., predecessors], so let them not impatiently urge Me.

ayahArabic: فَإِنَّ لِلَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا۟ ذَنُوبًا مِّثْلَ ذَنُوبِ أَصْحٰبِهِمْ فَلَا يَسْتَعْجِلُونِ
ayahEnglish: And indeed, for those who have wronged is a portion [of punishment] like the portion of their companions [i.e., predecessors], so let them not impatiently urge Me.

ayahArabic: فَوَيْلٌ لِّلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ مِن يَوْمِهِمُ ٱلَّذِى يُوعَدُونَ
ayahEnglish: And woe to those who have disbelieved from their Day which they are promised.

ayahArabic: فَوَيْلٌ لِّلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ مِن يَوْمِهِمُ ٱلَّذِى يُوعَدُونَ
ayahEnglish: And woe to those who have disbelieved from their Day which they are promised.

targetTranslation: the one who/which/that (refers to يَوْمِهِمُ - their day)
ayahArabic: فَوَيْلٌ لِّلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ مِن يَوْمِهِمُ ٱلَّذِى يُوعَدُونَ
ayahEnglish: And woe to those who have disbelieved from their Day which they are promised.

targetTranslation: they are being promised/warned (refers to those who disbelieved)
ayahArabic: فَوَيْلٌ لِّلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ مِن يَوْمِهِمُ ٱلَّذِى يُوعَدُونَ
ayahEnglish: And woe to those who have disbelieved from their Day which they are promised.
